title: SACRAMENTO, CA —

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California has enacted a law requiring social media platforms to disclose their content moderation policies and metrics regarding hate speech, disinformation, and extremism.",
    "The implementation of Assembly Bill 587 will provide greater transparency into how social media companies manage and moderate public discourse.",
    "California's new law aims to allow consumers and policymakers to determine whether platforms are effectively removing extreme content or amplifying it for engagement.",
    "California is leading the world with the most stringent transparency measures for Big Tech regarding social media content moderation.",
    "Numerous studies have linked online activity to hate-motivated violence and mass shootings.",
    "A Department of Homeland Security bulletin indicated that extremist, copycat behavior has been promoted in online forums following violent incidents.",
    "ADL's recent report highlights that Asian Americans have reported a significant increase in online harassment and that this correlates with offline incidents of anti-Asian hate.",
    "Studies have confirmed that social media has been a major contributor to the spread of public health disinformation.",
    "Research has shown that a small number of individuals and their organizations were responsible for a majority of vaccine misinformation on major social media platforms.",
    "Misinformation on social media has been linked to increased vaccine hesitancy and refusal during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Efforts by social media companies to manage harmful content have been criticized as grossly inadequate.",
    "Facebook's internal evidence suggests that its algorithms promote harmful content, fostering polarization and divisiveness."
  ]
}
```
